title: ClosedThesis
description: A thesis after position exit. Includes full attribution decomposition, assumption validation, and quality scores. This is the primary training data object for the feedback loop.

thesis_snapshot.description: Full Thesis object at time of close. Archived for immutability.

fx_rate_at_entry.description: EUR/USD (or relevant pair) at entry, if ETF is non-EUR.

attribution_confidence.description: Mark 'low' when holding_days < 60 or when sector_beta and catalyst_alignment are both > 80% (collinear).

outcome_quality.description: Investment OUTCOME score. Rubric (see scoring_weights.yaml closed_thesis_rubrics for full anchors): 0=loss>30%+stopped out, 1-2=loss 10-30%, 3=near breakeven/bad timing, 4=gain 0-5%, 5=gain 5-15%, 6=gain 15-30%+clean exit, 7=gain 30-50%+all assumptions validated, 8=gain 50-75%+full thesis delivery, 9=gain 75-100%, 10=gain>100%. Measures outcome, not reasoning — a lucky bad thesis can score 7.

thesis_quality.description: REASONING quality at thesis creation time, evaluated retroactively at close. Independent of outcome — a well-constructed thesis that lost money scores high here. Rubric (see scoring_weights.yaml closed_thesis_rubrics): 0-1=vague/untestable, 2-3=weak logic, 4=baseline, 5=all assumptions testable+clear falsification, 6=novel sector angle+tight falsification, 7=non-consensus catalyst when written, 8=identified catalyst before sell-side, 9=novel catalyst+non-consensus sector+clean falsification, 10=reserved for theses right for the exact right reason with full attribution.

matrix_cell.description: 2x2 matrix: confirmed=high thesis_quality + profit, bad_luck=high thesis_quality + loss, lucky=low thesis_quality + profit, avoided_future=low thesis_quality + loss. Threshold: thesis_quality >= 6 = high.

right_reason_score.description: COMPUTED — do not estimate. Formula (see scoring_weights.yaml closed_thesis_rubrics): (validated_assumptions/total_assumptions)×0.40 + (catalyst_materialized?0.35:0) + (alpha_from_catalyst?0.25:0). catalyst_materialized=true only if the primary mechanism described in thesis.catalyst actually occurred, not just a coincidental price move. alpha_from_catalyst=true if attribution.catalyst_alpha>0 AND attribution_confidence!='low'.

lessons.description: Specific, actionable lessons for future similar theses. Not generic observations.
